definition: To reduce in amount, degree, or intensity.
exampleSentence: The storm began to ___ after midnight.

definition: To renounce or give up a monarchical throne or responsibility.
exampleSentence: The king chose to ___ in favor of his daughter.

definition: A departure from what is normal or expected.
exampleSentence: The temperature spike was a seasonal ___.

definition: A state of temporary disuse or suspension.
exampleSentence: Matters were held in ___ until the leader returned.

definition: To regard with extreme disgust and hatred.
exampleSentence: Humanitarian workers ___ all forms of violence.

definition: To solemnly renounce or recant a belief or claim.
exampleSentence: He was forced to ___ his political views.

definition: To formally put an end to a system or practice.
exampleSentence: Congress voted to ___ the outdated tax law.
The officer moved to ___ the suspect.

definition: To shorten a book, speech, or right without losing main sense.
exampleSentence: An ___ version of the novel was published.

definition: To set free from blame, guilt, or obligation.
exampleSentence: The jury verdict served to ___ the defendant.

definition: To restrain oneself from doing or enjoying something.
exampleSentence: He decided to ___ from eating sugar.

definition: Not self-indulgent, especially when eating and drinking.
exampleSentence: Her ___ lifestyle contributed to her longevity.

definition: An award or privilege granted as a special honor.
exampleSentence: She received the highest ___ for her research.
They were forced to ___ to the demands.

definition: Angry and bitter in speech or relationship.
exampleSentence: The meeting turned into an ___ dispute.

definition: The ability to make good judgements and quick decisions.
exampleSentence: His business ___ led to profitable ventures.
She was ___ that she would not resign.

definition: Very skilled or proficient at something.
exampleSentence: He is ___ at solving intricate puzzles.

definition: To warn or reprimand someone firmly.
exampleSentence: The teacher had to ___ the loud students.
The actor soaked in the ___ of his fans.
She showed great resilience in the face of ___.

definition: Concerned with beauty or the appreciation of beauty.
Friendly, good-natured, or easy to talk to.
exampleSentence: The manager was an ___ person loved by all.

definition: Having a great deal of money; wealthy.
A whole formed by combining several separate elements.
exampleSentence: The ___ score determined the tournament winner.

definition: Make suffering, deficiency, or a problem less severe.
exampleSentence: The medicine will ___ your back pain.

definition: Not friendly or forthcoming; cool and distant.
exampleSentence: He remained ___ from the office gossip.

definition: Showing selfless concern for the well-being of others.
exampleSentence: Her ___ efforts helped hundreds of orphans.

definition: Combine or unite to form one organization or structure.
exampleSentence: The two credit unions decided to ___.

definition: Open to more than one interpretation; unclear.
Having mixed feelings or contradictory ideas about something.
exampleSentence: She felt ___ about moving to a new city.

definition: Make something bad or unsatisfactory better.
exampleSentence: Measures were taken to ___ working conditions.

definition: Open and responsive to suggestion; easily persuaded.
exampleSentence: The parents were ___ to the teacher's ideas.

definition: Having or displaying a friendly and pleasant manner.
exampleSentence: An ___ hostess welcomed all the guests.

definition: A thing belonging to a period other than that in which it exists.
exampleSentence: A sword in a futuristic sci-fi movie is an ___.

definition: Comparable in certain respects, typically in a way which makes clearer.
exampleSentence: The heart is ___ to a mechanical pump.

definition: A state of disorder due to absence or nonrecognition of authority.
exampleSentence: The collapse of government led to total ___.
There was palpable ___ between the rivals.

definition: Something that deviates from what is standard, normal, or expected.
exampleSentence: The test result was an unusual ___.

definition: A deep-seated feeling of dislike or aversion.
exampleSentence: She felt a strong ___ toward dishonest people.

definition: Lack of interest, enthusiasm, or concern.
exampleSentence: Voter ___ led to low turnout at elections.

definition: (of a story) of doubtful authenticity, although widely circulated.
exampleSentence: The tale of Newton and the apple may be ___.

definition: Pacify or placate someone by acceding to their demands.
exampleSentence: They tried to ___ the angry customer.

definition: Anxious or fearful that something bad or unpleasant will happen.
exampleSentence: She was ___ about her driving test.

definition: Involving or requiring strenuous effort; difficult and tiring.
exampleSentence: It was an ___ journey up the mountain.

definition: Having or showing the ability to speak fluently and coherently.
exampleSentence: She gave an ___ defense of her position.

definition: Characterized by severe self-discipline and abstention from all indulgence.
exampleSentence: Monks live an ___ life of prayer and simplicity.

definition: Make an unpleasant feeling less intense.
exampleSentence: Nothing could ___ her grief after the loss.

definition: Having or showing an ability to accurately assess situations.
exampleSentence: An ___ businessman spots great investments early.

definition: Showing a willingness to take surprisingly bold risks.
exampleSentence: The robbery was an ___ daytime heist.

definition: Severe or strict in manner, attitude, or appearance; plain.
exampleSentence: The room was furnished in an ___ fashion.

definition: Having the freedom or right to govern itself; independent.
Extreme greed for wealth or material gain.
exampleSentence: His unbridled ___ drove him to embezzle funds.
